title: Charred British Egg Rolls Spring Rolls
description: A light British dish featuring charred and chewy egg rolls, perfect for a snack or dinner, and egg-free friendly by using a tofu-based filling instead of traditional egg rolls

ingredients.name: spring roll wrappers
ingredients.notes: found in the frozen food section

instructions: Boil a large pot of water and blanch the shredded cabbage for 30 seconds to remove excess moisture.
In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil over medium heat and saute the chopped onion until translucent.
Add the grated carrots to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
Crumble the tofu into the skillet and stir to combine with the onion and carrot mixture.
Add the soy sauce, sesame oil, salt, and pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
To assemble the spring rolls, lay a sheet of spring roll wrapper on a flat surface and place 1/4 cup of the tofu mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Brush the edges of the wrapper with a small amount of water and fold the bottom half of the wrapper over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the wrapper to form a tight cylinder.
Repeat the assembly process with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Heat 1/4 cup of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and char the spring rolls for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and crispy.
Serve the charred spring rolls hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
